lesson.subtitle: Aula 12 -- Creditor Negotiations: Language of Compromise
lesson.title_tag: Professor View -- Eduarda | Aula 12 -- Creditor Negotiations

listenings.text: Thank you for the proposal. We have reviewed it carefully. Honestly, the current repayment schedule is a sticking point for us. We could accept a longer timeline, but only if the interest rate stays the same. We would also need a firm commitment on the first payment. If you could offer some additional security, that might help us reach a settlement that works for both sides.

Hi, this is a quick update on the creditor negotiations. Good news -- both sides have moved closer. They would accept a phased payment plan, and we could meet them halfway on the rate. There is still one sticking point: the deadline for the first installment. I think we might reach a settlement by Friday. Let us talk before the next call.

extra_audio.text: Both sides present their opening positions. Each side identifies its main sticking point. They explore possible trade-offs and concessions. A counteroffer is made to meet halfway. Both sides agree on a win-win settlement.
